data.title: Financial Advisors and Brokers to Drive Next Phase of Crypto ETF Adoption
data.description:
According to PANews, Bloomberg ETF analyst James Seyffart believes that the next phase of crypto asset ETF adoption will be driven by financial advisors managing assets for high-net-worth individuals, large brokerage firms, and brokers. In a recent episode of the 'Coin Stories' podcast, Seyffart discussed how these financial institutions, which manage trillions of dollars in assets, play a crucial role in expanding the BTC ETF market.

Seyffart noted that the performance of BTC ETFs in their first year has been exceptionally strong, surpassing many analysts' expectations. While Bloomberg had a positive outlook on BTC ETFs, the actual performance exceeded their forecasts. He mentioned that although there have been some outflows in recent weeks, the funds have only decreased by about $40 billion from their peak, with current assets totaling approximately $110 billion. The IBIT ETF has been one of the most actively traded, reaching $50 billion in just over 100 days, breaking previous records that took over a thousand days to achieve.

Given this momentum, Seyffart believes that using BTC ETFs as a tool for portfolio allocation for high-net-worth clients could drive their continued success. Despite recommendations from large companies like BlackRock to allocate 1% to 2% of portfolios to BTC, Seyffart emphasized that 'large brokerage firms and big banks' do not allow investors to purchase crypto asset ETFs.

He added that these brokers, financial advisors, and brokerage platforms control significant amounts of money, including funds from billionaires. These institutions influence asset allocation decisions across a wide range of financial portfolios. Seyffart suggested that if these institutions begin to include BTC ETFs as part of their portfolios, for example, at a 5% allocation, it could drive continued growth in adoption.

In addition to institutional adoption, Seyffart observed a trend of corporations, states, and even countries incorporating BTC into their balance sheets. This trend helps enhance the legitimacy and stability of BTC as an asset class within traditional finance.

However, he stressed that increased acceptance by financial intermediaries could be a key driver of ETF growth. Today, asset management firm Grayscale released a report claiming that high-net-worth investors are showing interest in crypto assets. Grayscale CEO Peter Mintzberg stated on social media, 'It's exciting to see the momentum shifting for crypto assets, with more investors recognizing the value of digital assets. Notably, 38% of high-net-worth investors believe their portfolios will include crypto assets in the future.'

data.title: A trader bought 254.66M $DOGINME yesterday. Today, due to the news from Coinbase, his book profit exceeded $110,000
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News According to Lookonchain monitoring, a trader created a new wallet yesterday and spent 51.2 ETH (about 97,700 US dollars) to purchase 254.66M $DOGINME.
Today, Coinbase added $DOGINME to its listing roadmap, and the price of the token soared by more than 150%. As of now, the value of $DOGINME held by the trader has reached 205,700 US dollars, with a paper profit of 108,000 US dollars (+110%).

data.title: Kevin Lee, Chief Business Officer of Gate.io: The era of 100-fold surge may become history, and the crypto market is moving towards a mature cycle
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Recently, Kevin Lee, Chief Business Officer of Gate.io, accepted an exclusive interview with Bitcoin.com and shared his insights on topics such as strategic cooperation with the F1 Red Bull Racing Team, structural changes in the crypto market, maturity of the global regulatory industry, AI-enabled transformation, and Gate.io's future layout. Speaking of the strategic cooperation between Gate.io and the F1 Red Bull Racing Team, Kevin emphasized that brand sponsorship should focus on long-term value precipitation rather than immediate conversion. F1 is one of the fastest-growing sports in the world. Gate.io hopes to strengthen users' brand awareness of "cryptocurrency = Gate.io" through its connection with the world's top event IPs.
Regarding industry trends, Kevin mentioned that the current crypto market is undergoing a structural change, and it may be difficult to have a hundred-fold market in the future. However, he pointed out that with the gradual improvement of the regulatory framework and the acceleration of the industry's compliance process, the crypto industry will enter a mature development cycle like traditional finance, and it is expected to usher in a new wave of large-scale adoption. Regarding security issues, Kevin believes that technical vulnerabilities are only part of the factor, and the biggest risk is still human error. With the founder's deep technical background, Gate.io has built a multi-layer security and defense system, and has always regarded encryption security as a top priority. Speaking of AI applications, Kevin predicts that the deep integration of AI and blockchain will be completed within five years, "AI and blockchain will naturally integrate into daily life, and AI will become second nature." Looking to the future, Kevin said that Gate.io will continue to optimize user experience and enhance market liquidity. From sports sponsorship to security compliance, Gate.io is bridging the gap between cryptocurrency and traditional finance at a steady pace to welcome a new round of growth in the industry.

data.title: Crypto News: US Court Approves 3AC’s $1.53 Billion Claim Against FTX, Escalating Bankruptcy Dispute
data.description: According to Cryptopolitan: A U.S. court has ruled in favor of Three Arrows Capital (3AC), approving its $1.53 billion claim against FTX. This decision marks a significant development in the ongoing legal battle over the collapsed exchange’s remaining assets.Key Rulings and DevelopmentsCourt Rejects FTX’s Objections: The ruling dismissed FTX’s debtors’ efforts to block 3AC’s claim expansion.FTX’s Record-Keeping Delays Blamed: The court found that FTX’s failure to provide timely financial records contributed to delays in 3AC’s claim process.Original $120 Million Claim Expanded: 3AC initially sought $120 million but later alleged breach of contract, unjust enrichment, and fiduciary duty violations, justifying the $1.53 billion demand.Liquidation Controversy: Court documents reveal that 3AC had $1.53 billion in assets on FTX, which were liquidated to cover a $1.3 billion debt.FTX Bankruptcy Battle Intensifies: This ruling further complicates the creditor claims dispute, as multiple entities fight over FTX’s remaining assets.

data.title: Secured Finance launches FIL-collateralized stablecoin USDFC to boost the liquidity of the lending market in the Filecoin ecosystem
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News According to Messari's latest quarterly report, in the fourth quarter of 2024, Secured Finance launched the FIL-collateralized stablecoin USDFC based on the Filecoin ecosystem. The stablecoin aims to introduce stability and liquidity support to the Filecoin ecosystem.

USDFC allows FIL holders and storage providers to obtain USD stablecoins using FIL tokens as collateral. This mechanism enables participants to obtain operating funds without having to sell FIL, effectively protecting the value of FIL and maintaining network participation.

The launch of this stablecoin will also significantly enhance the liquidity of the lending market in the Filecoin ecosystem and improve the efficiency of capital utilization in the entire ecosystem. The addition of this innovative financial tool will bring richer financial application scenarios to the Filecoin ecosystem and help promote the further development of the ecosystem.

The launch of USDFC marks an important step for Filecoin in the DeFi field, providing more flexible fund management options for ecosystem participants and injecting new vitality into the entire ecosystem.
